title: The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, with the Apocryphal books (Forshall–Madden 1850 publication set)
summary: The repository artifact is the pinned 2006 University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English XML encoding of Forshall and Madden's four-volume 1850 edition. The edition prints two distinct late-fourteenth-century Middle English Bible version traditions in parallel, together with prefatory epistles, the Later Version General Prologue, additional prologues, a table of lessons, and an extensive critical apparatus. It is a composite scholarly edition made from scoped base manuscripts and wider collation, not a transcription of one manuscript and not a complete Wyclif oeuvre. This graph keeps the edition, the two version-level intellectual streams, nine encoded text streams, named manuscript witnesses, U-M text and page-image layers, four Internet Archive facsimiles, four OCR derivatives, and four control-metadata objects separate. Rights are likewise layer-specific: the published 1850 text and editorial layer are public domain in the United States and United Kingdom, while modern encoding and control-file status, provider terms, and possibly unpublished witness-specific variants require separate treatment.
review_notes: The cataloging boundary is one four-volume 1850 publication set, represented as one numbered item, with two version-level Middle English streams and nine U-M GROUP/TEXT streams; repeated parallel PB milestones are not additional works or physical pages.

Forshall and Madden's manuscript statements are modeled at their stated scopes. Royal MS 1 C VIII is the edition's Later Version base throughout, but current scholarship does not treat it as a transparent universal Later Version witness.
The XML retains a very large variant apparatus in NOTE1 and NOTE2 elements. The apparatus is an edition layer and is not flattened into the medieval version entities.
Confident attribution of the two versions, the General Prologue, or specific translation portions to Wyclif, Hereford, or Purvey is not accepted as established current scholarship.

The four complete Internet Archive volume PDFs, OCR files, item metadata records, and scan-data files are cached. Canonical cache files and worktree paths were verified as hard links to the same inodes.
No complete public digital facsimile was verified for any principal manuscript witness. Two sample images for Douce 369 do not constitute a complete witness facsimile and were not cached.

Access, copyright in historical wording, rights in modern encodings or control files, and provider reproduction terms are distinct assertions. Public viewing does not itself establish a reusable license.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afz9170
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: File size is 20,920,815 bytes at the reviewed commit.
The CME repository root LICENSE applies the Unlicense to software; it does not expressly license the XML data, transcription, or markup.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item AFZ9170.0001.001
alternate_labels: U-M CME electronic text of the Forshall–Madden Bible
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR and Level-1 TEI encoding tied to 3,314 page images. It serializes title matter and nine principal text streams, retains a large variant apparatus, and leaves omitted front and back matter as page milestones without verbal OCR.
notes: The current U-M item page calls the item public domain and links text sections to page views.
The legacy XML header says copyright may apply; that conflict is preserved in the rights records rather than silently resolved.

entities.label: University of Michigan page-image set for AFZ9170.0001.001
entities.description: The page-image carrier described in the XML header as 3,314 600-dpi TIFF G4 images and exposed through page-view links from the current U-M text interface.
entities.reproduction_format: Historical 600-dpi TIFF G4 master images; current web page-view derivatives
notes: No stable complete image manifest or downloadable bundle was verified; automated retrieval of an exposed page-view route returned HTTP 403 during review.

entities.label: Forshall–Madden, The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, with the Apocryphal books (1850)
entities.description: A four-volume Oxford University Press edition that prints what the editors called an Earlier and a Later Version in parallel, with prologues, prefatory materials, a table of lessons, glossary, errata, and a manuscript-based critical apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: The Holy Bible, Containing the Old and New Testaments, with the Apocryphal Books, in the Earliest English Versions Made from the Latin Vulgate by John Wycliffe and His Followers, ed. Josiah Forshall and Frederic Madden, 4 vols. (Oxford: University Press, 1850).
bibliographic.title: The Holy Bible, Containing the Old and New Testaments, with the Apocryphal Books, in the Earliest English Versions Made from the Latin Vulgate by John Wycliffe and His Followers

notes: The title page's Wyclif attribution is retained as historical edition wording, not treated as a settled modern authorship claim.
The publication-set boundary includes four complete volumes even though U-M omits verbal transcription of some outer editorial matter.

entities.label: Middle English Bible, Earlier Version tradition
entities.description: An abstract version-level description for the comparatively literal late-fourteenth-century Middle English translation tradition printed in the edition's Earlier Version columns. The schema has no abstract-work entity type, so catalog_description is used explicitly as a non-documentary intellectual-layer proxy.
notes: This entity does not assert that all Earlier Version witnesses share one uniform wording or that the edition exhausts the tradition.

entities.label: Middle English Bible, Later Version tradition
entities.description: An abstract version-level description for the more fluent revision tradition printed in the edition's Later Version columns, including the General Prologue and associated prologue material at their separately evidenced scopes. The schema has no abstract-work entity type, so catalog_description is used as an intellectual-layer proxy.
notes: Attribution to John Purvey is not treated as established; Mary Dove reports no evidence for Purvey's authorship of the General Prologue.

entities.label: Latin Vulgate Bible tradition
entities.description: The Latin biblical textual tradition from which the Middle English versions were translated and against which revision continued; this is a tradition-level source description, not one identified Vulgate manuscript.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Oxford, MS 4
entities.description: A first-quarter fifteenth-century complete Earlier Version Old and New Testament witness used as a principal text source and collation witness by Forshall and Madden, and as one source for the table of lessons.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, MS Douce 370
entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century Earlier Version witness containing Genesis through 2 Chronicles and serving as Forshall and Madden's base from IV Kings through II Paralipomenon.

entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, MS Douce 369
entities.description: A two-part Earlier Version Bible manuscript. Forshall and Madden used the first part as base from I Esdras through Baruch 3:20, and the second part as base from Baruch 3:20 through Ezekiel 1:26 and from Ezekiel 32:23 through Acts 28:15.

holding.notes: The official catalog describes the first part as 1390s and the second as last quarter of the fourteenth century.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: last quarter of the fourteenth century to 1390s

notes: Two public sample images were found, not a complete public facsimile.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 1 B VI
entities.description: A fourteenth- to fifteenth-century vellum Earlier Version New Testament witness used as Forshall and Madden's base from Acts 28:15 to the end of that version's New Testament.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 1 C VIII
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century vellum Later Version Bible used throughout as Forshall and Madden's standard Later Version text. Its edition-base role does not establish that all of its readings are an unrevised universal Later Version form.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 1666
entities.description: A first-quarter fifteenth-century manuscript used as the base for the opening of the Later Version General Prologue through chapter XV, volume I page 57 line 3.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Mm.2.15
entities.description: A Later Version Bible with the General Prologue. Forshall and Madden used it for the remainder of the General Prologue after Harley 1666 and collated substantial Later Version biblical scopes from it.

holding.notes: Forshall and Madden describe a vellum folio manuscript in two columns, about 1430; Dove identifies it as siglum Q, containing Later Version Old Testament, Prologue, and New Testament.

entities.label: New College, Oxford, MS 66
entities.description: A 1415–1425 manuscript containing Later Version Genesis through Psalms and mixed Earlier/Later Version prologue material. Forshall and Madden used it for the Later Version Jerome epistles before Genesis.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 15580
entities.description: A fourteenth-century Earlier Version manuscript containing Proverbs through Apocalypse and a prefixed table of lessons; Forshall and Madden used it with Corpus Christi College MS 4 for the printed table.

Digital Bodleian two-image object for MS Douce 369
entities.description: The complete two-canvas Digital Bodleian object published from 35mm slides. Its images are fol. 303r, containing the prologue to Baruch and the opening of Baruch, and fol. 338r, containing the end of Nahum and opening of Habakkuk. It is complete as a provider object but only partial coverage of the 486-folio composite manuscript.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and two 1800-pixel-width JPEG derivatives
notes: Fol. 303r is in Douce 369 part 2 but precedes the edition's base switch at Baruch 3:20; it provides an overlapping/collation view, not the printed base for the opening of Baruch.
Fol. 338r falls within the part-2 span that supplies the edition's Earlier Version base from Ezekiel 32:23 through Acts 28:15.

entities.label: John Zachariah Shuster, A Hard Day for the Scribe of MS 66 (2018)
entities.description: A four-page New College Notes article describing New College MS 66 and reproducing a full image of fol. 211r plus two details of fol. 215r. Those images document the Later Version Psalms and corrections, not the Jerome-epistle source scope used directly in the edition.

bibliographic.citation: John Zachariah Shuster, ‘A Hard Day for the Scribe of MS 66, a Wycliffite Bible in New College, Oxford,’ New College Notes 10 (2018), no. 2, 1–4.
bibliographic.title: A Hard Day for the Scribe of MS 66, a Wycliffite Bible in New College, Oxford

entities.label: Walter W. Skeat, Twelve Facsimiles of Old English Manuscripts (1892)
entities.description: A complete printed palaeographical teaching book with twelve plates and transcriptions. Plate VIII reproduces one selected MS Douce 370 page and transcribes Earlier Version 2 Chronicles 6:34–7:9, cross-referenced to Forshall–Madden volume II page 399.

bibliographic.citation: Walter W. Skeat, Twelve Facsimiles of Old English Manuscripts, with Transcriptions and an Introduction (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1892).
bibliographic.title: Twelve Facsimiles of Old English Manuscripts, with Transcriptions and an Introduction

notes: The 1892 title page names Rev. Walter W. Skeat and identifies him as Elrington and Bosworth Professor of Anglo-Saxon at Cambridge; GND 115611622 independently names Skeat, Walter W., dates him 1835–1912, and describes the same Cambridge professorship, differentiating this author from the 1866–1953 namesake.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Skeat's Twelve Facsimiles (1892)
entities.description: A complete 68-page scan of Skeat's printed book. The scan is complete for that publication, while its plate VIII is only one selected reproduction from the physical MS Douce 370 witness.

entities.label: Mary Dove, The First English Bible (2007), public excerpt and index
entities.description: A current scholarly study of the Wycliffite Bible traditions. Complete copies of Cambridge's ten-page public excerpt and thirty-three-page index file are cached; together they remain partial coverage of the full copyrighted book.

bibliographic.citation: Mary Dove, The First English Bible: The Text and Context of the Wycliffite Versions (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 2007).
bibliographic.title: The First English Bible: The Text and Context of the Wycliffite Versions

notes: The mutable HTML frontmatter page is retained only as a bibliographic alternate URL and was not cached; the stable public excerpt and index PDFs were cached exactly.

entities.description: The complete current official circular used to verify United States term and public-domain timing for pre-1931 publications.

entities.label: GND authority record 116664266 for Josiah Forshall
entities.description: A differentiated-person authority record naming Forshall, Josiah, giving birth year 1795 and death year 1863, and linking current VIAF 22899508 and LCNAF n82048625 identifiers.

entities.label: GND authority record 119453185 for Frederic Madden
entities.description: A differentiated-person authority record naming Madden, Frederic, giving exact dates 16 February 1801–8 March 1873, and linking current VIAF 74608937 and LCNAF n79055059 identifiers.

entities.label: GND authority record 115611622 for Walter W. Skeat
entities.description: A differentiated-person authority record naming Skeat, Walter W., giving birth year 1835 and death year 1912, distinguishing him from the anthropologist Walter William Skeat (1866–1953), and linking current VIAF 4939197 and LCNAF n80032735 identifiers.

agents.name: Josiah Forshall
notes: The GND record establishes the differentiated person and lifespan; the 1850 title page independently reconciles that name to this edition's editor role.

agents.name: Frederic Madden
notes: The GND record establishes the differentiated person and exact lifespan; the 1850 title page independently reconciles that name to this edition's editor role.

agents.name: Anonymous Earlier Version translators
notes: Collective placeholder for anonymous historical agents, not a claim that they formed a named institution.

agents.name: Anonymous Later Version revisers
notes: Collective placeholder for anonymous historical agents; specific Wyclif, Hereford, and Purvey attributions remain disputed or part-scoped.

agents.name: Walter W. Skeat
notes: The GND record differentiates the philologist's identity and lifespan from the younger namesake anthropologist; the 1892 title page independently reconciles Walter W. Skeat to this exact publication's authorship.

rights.component: Underlying words and editorial matter published in 1850
rights.basis: The source edition was published in 1850, before the current United States public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Modern XML transcription, OCR corrections if any, selection, and markup
rights.basis: The CME root Unlicense expressly addresses software, while the legacy XML header says copyright may apply; no explicit reusable license for this XML data layer was verified.
notes: This uncertainty does not alter the public-domain status of the underlying 1850 words.

rights.component: Underlying edition text displayed by the current U-M item

editorial_practices.source_feature: A large, internally varied manuscript tradition represented through continuous and switching bases plus a critical apparatus.
editorial_practices.representation: The Later Version follows Royal MS 1 C VIII continuously; the Earlier Version switches among explicitly scoped bases, while named and many additional witnesses supply verbatim or variant collation.
editorial_practices.description: Forshall and Madden constructed critical texts rather than diplomatically transcribing one codex. Direct base spans, collation spans, and the apparatus are separate provenance roles.
editorial_practices.scope: Earlier and Later Version biblical texts and their printed apparatus across volumes I–IV
notes: The current graph records the named principal base and collation witnesses needed to explain the encoded edition. It does not yet exhaust the edition's full siglum-by-siglum apparatus census.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two version traditions, heterogeneous prologues, a table of lessons, and other editorial matter survive in different manuscript configurations.
editorial_practices.representation: The edition prints Earlier and Later Version biblical texts side by side, constructs the General Prologue from Harley 1666 and CUL Mm.2.15, uses New College 66 for stated prologue material, and constructs the table from Corpus Christi College MS 4 and Add MS 15580.
editorial_practices.description: The four-volume publication is a coordinated editorial compilation. Its parallel columns and ancillary streams do not collapse the two medieval version traditions into one work or make every source witness coextensive.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete four-volume publication set, especially prefatory and prologue material and the table of lessons

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 3,314-image source carrier contains the complete four-volume scan, including editorial outer matter.
editorial_practices.representation: The encoding supplies verbal OCR for title matter and nine principal text streams, but marks seventy-two pages of omitted front matter and fifty-four pages of omitted back matter only with PB milestones.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M electronic text is not a full verbal transcription of every scanned page. Its omissions are encoding-layer selection, not evidence that the printed publication lacks those pages.
editorial_practices.scope: Outer FRONT and BACK wrappers and the nine GROUP/TEXT streams

editorial_practices.source_feature: Forshall and Madden's extensive printed manuscript-variant apparatus.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML retains the apparatus primarily in 98,765 NOTE1 and 671 NOTE2 elements alongside the parallel prose text.
editorial_practices.description: The electronic encoding preserves the edition's apparatus as a distinct high-volume layer; it does not convert each recorded reading into a separately cataloged medieval work or witness entity.
editorial_practices.scope: Encoded biblical and prologue text across all nine principal streams

evidence.citation: General Corpus / pinned CME source XML for afz9170

notes: Pinned CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c; git blob b6f31e1f39fa194535b2a1481313a2e1a930f11f; 20,920,815 bytes.

evidence.citation: Structural and textual inspection of afz9170 XML
evidence.locator: outer FRONT and BACK; GROUP/TEXT 1–9; element counts; PB REF and SEQ inventories
evidence.summary: Verifies the one-publication-set/nine-stream boundary; the four Earlier and five Later Version streams; prose form; 98,765 NOTE1 and 671 NOTE2 apparatus elements; omitted-matter wrappers; 6,405 PB occurrences; and 3,314 unique PB REF values.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at the pinned submodule commit
evidence.summary: Applies the Unlicense to software and therefore does not expressly resolve rights in the XML data, transcription, or markup layer.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, AFZ9170.0001.001, The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, with the Apocryphal books
evidence.summary: Confirms the stable item identity, 1850 publication, publicly searchable full text, current Public Domain / No Copyright–United States assertion, and page-view links from text sections.
notes: Mutable provider HTML was not cached. The pinned repository artifact supplies the stable XML evidence object.

evidence.citation: Inspection of U-M section page and exposed page-view route for AFZ9170.0001.001
evidence.locator: View Page 104 links and corresponding page-view path
evidence.summary: Confirms that the current section interface exposes page-view links. Automated retrieval of a linked image route returned HTTP 403, and no complete stable manifest or image inventory was verified.
notes: Mutable provider HTML and the failed response were not cached.

evidence.citation: Josiah Forshall and Frederic Madden, eds., The Holy Bible, vol. I (Oxford: University Press, 1850)
evidence.locator: title page; preface; manuscript table; General Prologue and parallel Genesis–Ruth text
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for edition identity, responsibility, historical attribution theory, editorial method, source witnesses, and volume-I contents.

evidence.citation: Josiah Forshall and Frederic Madden, eds., The Holy Bible, vol. II (Oxford: University Press, 1850)
evidence.locator: parallel Earlier and Later Version text, I Kings–Psalms; Earlier Version 2 Chronicles 6:34–7:9 at p. 399
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the second-volume boundary and parallel biblical text, including the passage reproduced in Skeat plate VIII.

evidence.citation: Josiah Forshall and Frederic Madden, eds., The Holy Bible, vol. III (Oxford: University Press, 1850)
evidence.locator: parallel Earlier and Later Version text, Proverbs–II Maccabees
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the third-volume boundary and parallel biblical text.

evidence.citation: Josiah Forshall and Frederic Madden, eds., The Holy Bible, vol. IV (Oxford: University Press, 1850)
evidence.locator: parallel New Testaments; additional prologues; table of lessons; glossary and errata
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the fourth-volume boundary, New Testament streams, additional prologues, table, and terminal editorial matter.

evidence.citation: Forshall and Madden, volume I preface and List of Manuscripts
evidence.locator: preface paragraphs 61–64; Earlier and Later Version source table; manuscript descriptions
evidence.summary: States the exact Earlier Version base switches, Royal MS 1 C VIII as continuous Later Version base, verbatim and variant collation roles, two-source General Prologue and table construction, New College 66 prologue use, and the larger manuscript census.
notes: Exact OCR verification: Corpus Christi Oxford MS 4 = Genesis–III Kings and Ezekiel 1:26–32:23; Douce 370 = IV Kings–II Paralipomenon; Douce 369 part 1 = I Esdras–Baruch 3:20; Douce 369 part 2 = Baruch 3:20–Ezekiel 1:26 and Ezekiel 32:23–Deeds/Acts 28:15; Royal 1 B VI = Acts 28:15–end; Royal 1 C VIII = Later Version throughout.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for holybiblecontain01wycluoft
evidence.summary: Confirms identifier, volume 1, date 1850, Robarts contribution, uoft1 scanning, 2007 public date, NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT provider status, and absence of a rights or license URL.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for holybiblecontain02wycluoft
evidence.summary: Confirms identifier, volume 2, date 1850, Robarts contribution, uoft1 scanning, 2007 public date, NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT provider status, and absence of a rights or license URL.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for holybiblecontain03wycluoft
evidence.summary: Confirms identifier, volume 3, date 1850, Robarts contribution, uoft1 scanning, 2007 public date, NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT provider status, and absence of a rights or license URL.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for holybiblecontain04wycluoft
evidence.summary: Confirms identifier, volume 4, date 1850, Robarts contribution, uoft1 scanning, 2007 public date, NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT provider status, and absence of a rights or license URL.

evidence.citation: Henry Ansgar Kelly, ‘Bible Translation and Controversy in Late Medieval England,’ in A Companion to Medieval Translation (Arc Humanities Press, 2019), 51–62
evidence.summary: Supports neutral Middle English Bible terminology, broad 1370s/1380s dating, the literal-to-fluent relation between versions, and present rejection of confident Wyclif, Hereford, and Purvey attributions.
notes: The DOI is stable, but no authorized stable public chapter PDF was verified; no local copy was cached.

evidence.citation: Mary Dove, The First English Bible: The Text and Context of the Wycliffite Versions (Cambridge University Press, 2007), public excerpt
evidence.locator: introduction pp. 2–5; public index of manuscripts
evidence.summary: Documents the large manuscript tradition, the edition's side-by-side version distinction, lack of evidence for Purvey's General-Prologue authorship, ongoing revision, and Royal MS 1 C VIII's partly revised Later Version state. The separately cached index supplies exact witness/siglum entries.
notes: The public excerpt and index PDFs are cached. The bibliographic HTML frontmatter page is mutable and was not cached.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval-manuscripts records for MSS Douce 369, Douce 370, Corpus Christi College Oxford 4, and New College Oxford 66 at commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmarks, dates, contents, Forshall–Madden sigla and source scopes, Douce 369's two-part contents and exact folio ranges, and New College 66's mixed Earlier/Later Version prologue material.
notes: Complete pinned TEI records for all four witnesses are separately represented as entities and local copies; the evidence repository_path names the first of those four cached records.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts records for Royal MSS 1 B VI and 1 C VIII, Harley MS 1666, and Add MS 15580
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmarks, contents, dates, physical extents, Royal 1 C VIII's standard revised-version role, Harley 1666's General Prologue, and Add 15580's Earlier Version and prefixed table of lessons.
notes: Current catalog HTML is mutable and was not cached; no stable public record export or complete digitised-content link was verified.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Archives and Modern and Medieval Manuscripts
evidence.summary: Establishes the current institutional enquiry route for manuscript consultation. The exact Mm.2.15 identity and contents are supported by Forshall–Madden and Dove pending an item-level current catalog record.
notes: Mutable institutional HTML was not cached.

evidence.citation: Wycliffite Bible Digital Edition, Charles University
evidence.summary: Provides a current scholarly project description: a complete English Bible translation produced probably at Oxford by followers of John Wyclif near the end of the fourteenth century.
notes: Mutable project HTML was not cached.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Bodleian Library MS Douce 369, two-image IIIF object 52b725a6-eb3e-40e4-b125-a137a5aed11b
evidence.summary: The exact manifest contains two canvases only, labels them fols. 303r and 338r, identifies the composite manuscript, and states © Bodleian Libraries with CC BY-NC 4.0 and Digital Bodleian terms. Direct inspection maps the images to Baruch and Nahum/Habakkuk.

evidence.citation: John Zachariah Shuster, ‘A Hard Day for the Scribe of MS 66, a Wycliffite Bible in New College, Oxford,’ New College Notes 10 (2018), no. 2, 1–4
evidence.locator: p. 1 fol. 211r; pp. 3–4 details of fol. 215r
evidence.summary: Describes MS 66 as an early-fifteenth-century Later Version Old Testament from Genesis through Psalms, discusses scribal corrections, and reproduces selected Psalter surfaces. Those images are not the edition's direct Jerome-epistle source scope.

evidence.citation: Walter W. Skeat, Twelve Facsimiles of Old English Manuscripts (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1892), complete Internet Archive/Cornell scan
evidence.locator: title page; plate VIII and transcription p. 28; PDF pages 46–47
evidence.summary: The complete printed supporting book reproduces one Douce 370 page at plate VIII, identifies Earlier Version 2 Chronicles 6:34–7:9 and Forshall–Madden volume II page 399, and states no known U.S. copyright restrictions on the text.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Copyright Term and the Public Domain, Circular 15A
evidence.summary: Supports the 2026 conclusion that works published before 1931, including the 1850 and 1892 publications, are in the United States public domain.
notes: The local file is hard-linked to identical bytes already present in another corpus cache; no duplicate storage is consumed.

evidence.citation: 17 U.S.C. § 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works
evidence.summary: States that copyright protection under Title 17 is not available for works of the United States Government, supporting the circular's public-domain status.
notes: Official mutable HTML was not cached; the circular itself is cached as the evidence object used for the edition-term analysis.

evidence.citation: United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright (term)
evidence.summary: States the life-plus-seventy literary-work term, seventy-year rules for unknown authors, twenty-five-year typographical-arrangement term, and the possible 31 December 2039 expiry for certain old works unpublished at the end of 1988.
notes: Official mutable HTML was not cached.

evidence.citation: Targeted current catalog and public-facsimile search for the nine named principal manuscript witnesses
evidence.summary: No complete public facsimile was verified for any of the nine principal witnesses. Digital Bodleian publishes exactly two Douce 369 images; Skeat publishes one Douce 370 plate; the Shuster article publishes selected MS 66 Psalter surfaces; these partial reproductions are separately modeled and cached.
notes: Search-result and provider HTML are mutable and were not cached; stable downloadable image/PDF objects actually used were cached in full.

evidence.citation: German National Library, GND differentiated-person authority record 116664266, Josiah Forshall
evidence.summary: The complete GND JSON-LD record names Forshall, Josiah, gives 1795–1863, classifies the subject as a differentiated person, and links VIAF 22899508 and LCNAF n82048625. The separately inspected 1850 title page reconciles that authority identity to the edition's named editor Josiah Forshall.
notes: The record supplies CC0 1.0 rights metadata. Direct VIAF JSON retrieval was blocked by HTTP 403 and was not cached; current VIAF and LC identifiers are taken from this GND record.

evidence.citation: German National Library, GND differentiated-person authority record 119453185, Frederic Madden
evidence.summary: The complete GND JSON-LD record names Madden, Frederic, gives exact dates 16 February 1801–8 March 1873, classifies the subject as a differentiated person, and links VIAF 74608937 and LCNAF n79055059. The separately inspected 1850 title page reconciles that authority identity to the edition's named editor Frederic Madden.
notes: The record supplies CC0 1.0 rights metadata. Direct VIAF JSON retrieval was blocked by HTTP 403 and was not cached; current VIAF and LC identifiers are taken from this GND record.

evidence.citation: German National Library, GND differentiated-person authority record 115611622, Walter W. Skeat
evidence.summary: The complete GND JSON-LD record names Skeat, Walter W., gives 1835–1912, classifies the subject as a differentiated person, describes an Anglo-Saxon professorship at Cambridge, and links VIAF 4939197 and LCNAF n80032735. The 1892 title page names Walter W. Skeat and the same Cambridge professorship, reconciling GND 115611622 to this publication and differentiating him from the GND 128803150 namesake born in 1866.
notes: The record supplies CC0 1.0 rights metadata. GND 128803150 is cited only to document the younger namesake distinction; its record is not used for the edition or rights conclusion and was not cached.

evidence.citation: Local source-cache integrity, format, and hard-link audit for afz9170
evidence.summary: Verifies all 29 manifest-recorded local copies: four edition PDFs, four OCR texts, four IA metadata JSON files, four scandata XML files, the two-image IIIF ZIP, New College Notes PDF, Skeat PDF, Dove excerpt and index PDFs, four pinned Bodleian catalog XML files, USCO Circular 15A, and three GND JSON-LD authority records. Canonical and worktree paths have matching device/inode pairs; the USCO file also hard-links to identical existing corpus bytes.
notes: Eight PDFs passed qpdf checks without warnings. The exact provider bytes of USCO Circular 15A produced warning-only linearization hint-table diagnostics while qpdf reported that the operation succeeded; the valid provider file was retained without repair. JSON and JSON-LD parsed with jq; XML parsed with xmllint; the IIIF ZIP contains its exact source manifest, inventory, and all two images; hashes and byte counts match every local_copies entry.

open_questions.question: What are the exact current shelfmarks, survival states, and item-level catalog records for every manuscript and private source separately listed in Forshall and Madden's Earlier Version, Later Version, prologue, table, and variant apparatus?
next_steps: Build a siglum-by-siglum concordance from all four volumes through current specialist scholarship and catalogs, preserving each witness's exact continuous-base, scoped-transcription, collation, or apparatus role.

open_questions.question: What translator and reviser responsibilities can current specialist scholarship assign at book or passage level without reviving the superseded blanket Wyclif–Hereford–Purvey model?
next_steps: Review current book-level linguistic and textual studies and preserve manuscript attributions and modern reconstructions as qualified, part-scoped claims rather than names inherited from the 1850 title page.

open_questions.question: Can every source behind the volume-IV additional prologues be mapped to a current holding, especially the 1424 Granger manuscript known to the editors through Joseph Ames's transcript and the privately held Acland source?
next_steps: Trace historical ownership and sale records and compare current manuscript indices before adding direct source relations.

open_questions.question: Is a stable complete download or auditable inventory currently exposed for the 3,314 U-M page images, and what is the exact current image-delivery format and size?
next_steps: Retry the page-view routes in an interactive browser or ask U-M Digital Collections; automated requests to image-view paths returned HTTP 403, so do not invent a bundle size or completeness claim.

open_questions.question: What explicit reuse terms govern the U-M Level-1 TEI structure and OCR corrections, separately from the public-domain 1850 text and from the repository copy?
next_steps: Request item-specific rights clarification from U-M and Kyvero Press before treating encoding-specific markup or corrections as openly licensed.

open_questions.question: Which witness-specific readings were not published in the 1850 text or apparatus, or elsewhere before 1989, and may therefore remain subject in the United Kingdom to the special term for old works unpublished at the end of 1988?
next_steps: Perform a variant-by-variant publication-history and authorship audit before making a United Kingdom reuse claim for unprinted manuscript-specific readings; retain the separate 31 December 2039 caveat meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Are complete public facsimiles or clearly licensed current reproduction routes available for any of the nine named principal manuscript witnesses?
next_steps: Recheck holding-library catalogs and contact repositories for full-object access and reuse terms; preserve the verified Douce 369, Douce 370, and MS 66 images as partial reproductions only.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current item-level Cambridge University Library catalog identity and reproduction route for MS Mm.2.15?
next_steps: Ask Cambridge University Library for the item-level record and image-service terms before adding a catalog entity or public facsimile access claim.
